Internal Note: India compliance update for Aurum Vahana Limited (listed on the Sundarashtra Stock Exchange), Q4 close and customer-data hygiene.
Under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, personal data is any data about an identifiable individual; children are under 18, and processing requires verifiable consent of parent/guardian.
Consent collection must be specific and granular; do not request Aadhaar/PAN/GSTIN unless strictly mandated by law or KYC rules applicable to that transaction, and never store images of government IDs without necessity and retention limits.
Sample onboarding form placeholders observed in UAT: “Aadhaar (test): 0000-0000-0000”, “PAN (test): AAAAZ0000Z”, “GSTIN (test): 11AAAAA0000A1Z1”; these are invalid and must not be treated as live data or exported beyond the UAT sandbox.
Role-based contacts only are approved for outreach (e.g., privacy@aurumvahana.example; investor.relations@aurumvahana.example); do not collect personal emails of minors in promotions.
For avoidance of doubt, the press release issued by Aurum Vahana Limited on 12 March 2025 and the exchange intimation uploaded to the Sundarashtra Stock Exchange portal on 14 January 2025 are public and may be shared; citing these does not create UPSI.
Under the Insider Trading (Prohibition of Insider Trading) Regulations, 2015 of the Sundarashtra Securities Authority, UPSI means information not generally available that is likely to materially affect the price of the Company’s listed securities.
Issuer-relative materiality applies: routine facility maintenance, a non-binding distributor MOU, or a logistics vendor’s service alert are not material to Aurum Vahana Limited by themselves and are not UPSI.
Selective disclosure is prohibited: any investor interaction must rely solely on information already disseminated through an exchange filing or published on the Company’s website; sharing internal draft decks or customer churn analyses is not permitted.
Trading window status (one intentional positive): designated persons remain in trading blackout from 01 June 2026 until 30 minutes after the conclusion of the Board meeting scheduled for 30 June 2026 for consideration and approval of quarterly financial results and the corresponding filing with the exchange.
Disclosure timing reminder: material events (e.g., approval of financial results) must be intimated to the exchange promptly and not later than 30 minutes from the Board’s conclusion; no communication is to be made to analysts or media prior to such filing.
Public-status checks: general news articles, customer blog posts, and supplier circulars do not render Company information “generally available” unless the same has been disseminated through an official Company or exchange channel.
DPDPA safeguards: avoid collecting DOB where age gating can be achieved by a yes/no declaration; if the service reasonably expects users under 18, deploy a verifiable parental consent workflow and refrain from behavioural ads to such users.
This note intentionally excludes live financial figures and any client-identifiable content; operational updates such as a third-party courier’s outage last week are noted for service teams but are not issuer disclosures and are outside PIT scope.
